Title: Brand Perception and Sentiment Analysis of Insurance Companies.
Description: This project explores consumer sentiment and brand health within the insurance industry by analyzing social media discourse and customer feedback.
My Contribution: In this project, I spearheaded the end-to-end data pipeline, beginning with the comprehensive cleaning and preprocessing of unstructured textual data gathered from diverse insurance discussions. I implemented sentiment classification models to accurately categorize customer feedback into distinct emotional states, allowing for a nuanced understanding of public opinion. Additionally, I developed comparative visualizations to map brand perception across competing firms and conducted deep-dive keyword analysis to pinpoint specific operational pain points, such as delays in claims processing and customer support friction.
Tools Used: Python, Pandas for data manipulation, NLTKNLP, Matplotlib/Seaborn, and Jupyter Notebooks.

Title: Predictive Modeling of Student Academic Performance
Description: In this project, the objective is to develop a machine learning model that predicts whether a student is at risk of failure using the features in the data as input variables.
My Contribution: I applied statistical knowlede to understanding the distribution of the target feature hereby creating a new feature base on this distribution interpretation. I further developed the model and provided the interpretation report.
Tools Used: Python, Statistical Distribution, Pandas, Random Forest, Classification Models
